div.p: To all sons of holy Mother Church, both present and to come, William de Morville sends greeting. Know me to have granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the freedom and licence to plough and sow the whole land of Blainslie, and the whole plain around the outside of the wood down to the Leader, and to make good use of it according to their will between its boundaries, except for clearing woodland, as far as 'Windeslawe' and from there by the great road which descends by 'Windeslawe' towards Lauder, down to the boundaries of Lauder, just as my father Richard de Morville gave them in free alms. This foresaid liberty and licence I have granted to them at the request of my father, and for the salvation of the souls of David and Malcolm, late kings of the Scots, my lords, and for the salvation of William, king of the Scots, my lord, and for the salvation of my own soul, and of the soul of my wife and of all my ancestors and successors. Wherefore I will that the foresaid monks should have and possess the previously named liberty and licence as freely and quitely in everlasting alms, just as they possess any alms freely and quitely and honourably. With these witnesses: Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Richard de Morville, my father; Avice of Lancaster, my mother; Herbert, dean of Glasgow; Clement the physician, chaplain; Adam, parson of the church of Lauder; Roger the clerk, my father's steward; Alan of Thirlestane; William Mansell, fintor [?founder or ?surveyor of boundaries*]; Herbert son of Robert; Alan of Clapham.
*fintor is not attested; finitor is otherwise only attested in Classical Latin; funtor, 'founder', is the most likely reading.
